---
format: html
title: prepare_community_for_fit()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>prepare_community_for_fit</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Prepare Community Data for Model Fitting</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Reshapes long-format community data into a wide matrix suitable
for model fitting, filtering to the canonical sample index
supplied by 'align_sample_ids()'.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>prepare_community_for_fit(data_community_long = NULL, data_sample_ids = NULL)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_community_long">data_community_long</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame in long format with columns 'dataset_name', 'age',
'taxon', and 'pollen_prop'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_sample_ids">data_sample_ids</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame of valid '(dataset_name, age)' pairs as returned by
'align_sample_ids()'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>Only samples present in 'data_sample_ids' are retained. Rows
with 'NA' or zero 'pollen_prop' are dropped before pivoting.
The function widens the data and converts the result to a matrix
directly, so the output is ready for 'filter_constant_taxa()'
and 'assemble_data_to_fit()'.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A numeric matrix with row names in the format
'&quot;&lt;dataset_name&gt;__&lt;age&gt;&quot;' and taxon names as column names.
Missing taxon–sample combinations are filled with '0'. Rows are
ordered by 'dataset_name' then 'age', matching the order in
'data_sample_ids'.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[align_sample_ids()], [filter_constant_taxa()],
[assemble_data_to_fit()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
